changes: OpenClaw shipped newer stable release v2026.4.29, published on 2026-04-30 21:01 UTC.
Hermes Agent shipped v2026.4.30 on 2026-04-30 18:31 UTC, extending the learning-loop lane with another weekly release.
ZeroClaw shipped v0.7.4 on 2026-05-01 02:52 UTC, keeping the Rust lightweight line active.
PicoClaw shipped stable v0.2.8 on 2026-04-30 15:15 UTC and also pushed a fresh nightly on 2026-05-01 01:17 UTC.
nanobot did not add a newer release beyond v0.1.5.post3, but it remains the fastest-moving high-signal lightweight rewrite in the set.
whyItMatters: OpenClaw shipping again matters most because the category anchor is still releasing on a very tight cadence.
Hermes shipping the same day matters because the strongest adjacent benchmark is keeping pace on product momentum, not just mindshare.
ZeroClaw and PicoClaw shipping together reinforces that the lightweight and low-overhead lane is still the densest source of direct competitive pressure.
There is still no material taxonomy shift: the durable ecosystem map stays the same even as release velocity stays high.
Net: this was a meaningful run because multiple Tier 1 names shipped within roughly 24 hours, but the strategic read still holds.
